label: A2 — Elementary
blurb: 5 partes, 25 preguntas: imágenes, completar, opción múltiple y emparejar.

audios.title: Part 1 · Questions 1–5
audios.intro: For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C). You will hear five short conversations. Use the arrow ▶ or the numbers below to move to the next conversation.
scripts: Question one. Girl: Guess what I saw at the park this morning! Boy: A big dog? Girl: No — three little rabbits under a tree! Boy: Let's go and look after school.
Question two. Boy: What did you buy at the pet shop, Emma? Girl: I looked at a funny little hat for my cat, but I bought her a ball. She lost her old one under the sofa. Boy: She'll love it.
Question three. Mum: Are you taking the dog for his walk now? Boy: Look outside — it's snowing! Mum: He loves the snow. Put on your warm coat.
Question four. Man: What time do we take the cat to the vet? Woman: The appointment was at four, but they called — it's now half past four. Man: OK, we have more time then.
Question five. Girl: Are we cycling to the animal farm on Saturday? Boy: It's too far by bike. Dad said he'll take us in the car. Girl: Even better!

questions.q: Where did the girl see the rabbits?
What did Emma buy for her cat?
What is the weather like now?
What time is the vet appointment now?
How will they go to the animal farm?

audios.title: Part 2 · Questions 6–10
audios.intro: You will hear a teacher telling students about a class visit to the zoo. For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one word or a number or a date or a time.
scripts: Listen to a teacher talking about a class visit to the zoo. Teacher: Here is everything you need to know about our zoo visit on Friday. The bus leaves school at nine o'clock, so don't be late. The ticket costs seven pounds — please bring the money in an envelope with your name on it. At midday we'll watch the penguins at feeding time; that's my favourite part. Bring a warm coat, because most of the animals are outside. We'll be back at school at three o'clock.

audios.title: Part 3 · Questions 11–15
audios.intro: You will hear Lily talking to her friend Jack about her new pet. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
scripts: Jack: Lily, I heard you got a new pet! Is it a dog? Lily: I really wanted a dog, but our flat is too small, so we got a hamster. Jack: What's his name? Lily: Biscuit — because he's exactly that colour. Jack: Who looks after him? Lily: I clean his cage every Sunday, and my little brother gives him his food in the mornings. Jack: Does he sleep all day? Lily: Yes! He only wakes up in the evening, and then he runs and runs in his wheel. Jack: Can I come and see him? Lily: Sure, come on Saturday afternoon.

questions.q: Why didn't Lily get a dog?
Why is the hamster called Biscuit?
o: He is the colour of a biscuit

questions.q: When does Lily clean the cage?
What does the hamster do in the evening?

audios.title: Part 4 · Questions 16–20
audios.intro: You will hear a girl called Elena talking about helping at an animal home. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
scripts: Elena: Every Saturday morning I help at the animal home near my house. I started because my neighbour works there and she invited me. My job is to brush the dogs and fill their water bowls — I'm still too young to walk the big ones. My favourite dog is an old brown one called Charlie. Nobody wanted him for a whole year, because he isn't young or pretty. But last month a kind family finally took him home, and I was happy and sad at the same time. When I'm older, I want to be a vet.

questions.q: When does Elena help at the animal home?
Who invited Elena to help there?

o: Brushing dogs and filling water bowls

questions.q: Why did nobody want Charlie for a year?
What does Elena want to be when she's older?

audios.title: Part 5 · Questions 21–25
audios.intro: You will hear Tom talking about his friends' pets. Which animal does each person have? For each question, choose the correct answer.
scripts: Tom: Everyone in my group of friends has a different pet. Grace has a white cat that sleeps on her homework. Ben has a big friendly dog called Rocket. Nora keeps three orange fish in a tank in her bedroom. Ethan has a rabbit that lives in a little house in the garden. And Sandy has a green bird that says hello when you walk in.
